content:
Leonardo Grando is a Ph.D. Student at the University of Campinas (UNICAMP) in Brazil. I am interested in complex systems, agent-based simulation, artificial intelligence, the Internet of Things, programming, and machine learning tools. I have expertise in Netlogo, Python, R, Latex, SQL, and Linux tools.
My Ph.D. work project is an IoT devices (UAVs) swarm agent-based modeling simulation (ABMS) aiming the perpetual flight. The workflow is Netlogo to ABMS simulate, Python and R to data analysis, and I use Latex for my thesis writing.
I started my career change from the automotive industry as a productivity improvement professional to the academic and data science field in 2019. After this career change, I worked as a teacher in fundamental and high school and as a teaching intern at the undergraduate level.
Download my resumé .
summary: Leonardo Grando is a Ph.D. Student at the University of Campinas (UNICAMP) in Brazil. I am interested in complex systems, agent-based simulation, artificial intelligence, the Internet of Things, programming, and machine learning tools.
title: Leonardo Grando

content:
Follow below some internet resources list that I use to learn. This post is a curated and organic list.
First, a great thanks to all resources responsible cited in this list for sharing their knowledge with us. Any resource suggestions or corrections, let me know. Have a great learning. Get informed: Social news website about computing and entrepreneurship: Hacker News News source of technology: Slashdot General Computing/Programming Learning: Several programming courses and certifications: FreeCodeCamp Brazilian Group for new programming learners with challenges and Telegram support chat: OsProgramadores Brazilian computational thinking portal: Computacional Northwestern University Computational tools using Netlogo: CT-STEM Block program language cloud-based tool. Create stories, games and animations: MIT Scratch Create IPhone and Android apps using block program language: MIT App Inventor Machine Learning / Artificial inteligence: Great machine learning summary: Machine Learning for Everyone Great machine learning plain-english road-map: Machine learning for humans Machine learning and Data Science Community, competition, and Courses: Kaggle Brazilian data science community, with a great Slack community: Data Hackers Books/booksmarks lists: R language bookmark: Big book of R
Computer books list: Online Programming Books
Allen Downey books: Green Tea Press
Revision control:
v1 - 22/10/2022 - List started.
summary: This post is curate some good resources helping new and experienced learners
title: Learning Resource Links

summary: This work seeks to address one of the most critical problems of Flying Ad Hoc Networks (FANET), which is the issue of recharging batteries coordination. For recharges to be carried out in the best possible way, the number of load devices (Base Stations) should not be excessively high so as not to burden the network. On the other hand, it is also necessary that when the drones want to recharge, there must always be a source of energy available. For this, we propose internal estimators that provide intelligence to the drones to try to predict the next charger attendance rate. The drones will not have communication with each other to recharge coordination but will continue to communicate concerning other routine activities (note that this communication is not considered in the scope of this model), that is, for recharging the batteries’ coordination, there will be no energy expenditure on communication. The verification of the suitability of the proposal is done through Agent-Based Simulation and the use of three different policies for decision making. This will enable an approach that aims to optimize the operation of the system through a Nash equilibrium.
title: Drones Swarm Recharging: Modeling Using Agent-Based Simulation
